creator: gavinchappell303@hotmail.com
text:
From what I can gather, python 2.5 is listed as a runtime dependency of 
denyhosts (it was certainly pulled in on my previously python-less system) 
so even if I prefer to use an older/newer version of python as a system-wide 
default then denyhosts seems to be built and tested against 2.5.

In which case I don't see the harm in fixing denyhosts to use the 
/usr/local/bin/python2.5 binary, leaving /usr/local/bin/python to be 
symlinked to 2.4, 2.5, 2.6 or any other arbitrary version to define the 
default. At the point where denyhosts evolves too much to run on 2.5, then 
the dependency info would be changed to python2.6 and the 
command_interpreter set accordingly in the new initscript? People who 
upgrade and already have python 2.6 installed will already have a 
/usr/local/bin/python2.6 binary to use, people who only have 2.5 would get 
2.6 installed, 2.5 would be marked as unused if nothing else depended on it, 
and could then be removed when the user next does a package cleanup.

--------------------------------------------------
From: <itetcu@FreeBSD.org>
Sent: Wednesday, March 18, 2009 4:56 PM
To: <gavinchappell303@hotmail.com>; <itetcu@FreeBSD.org>; 
<freebsd-ports-bugs@FreeBSD.org>
Subject: Re: ports/132607: security/denyhosts: command_interpreter warnings 
in /var/log/messages

> Synopsis: security/denyhosts: command_interpreter warnings in 
> /var/log/messages
>
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback

> State-Changed-When: Wed Mar 18 16:56:14 UTC 2009
> State-Changed-Why:
> The fix is incorrent, I'd say: what if you use an other python version
> that 2.5?
>
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=132607
>
